compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## What

Add **lease-based self-healing** on top of the C1 ref-lock primitive so a crashed pass-holder
never wedges the reconciler beyond one lease interval. Builds directly on C1.

**Depends on: 524d-00fa-66ef-4432 (C1).** C1 delivers the `_ref_lock.py` module, the blob schema
(`heartbeat_ns`, `fence`), the `acquire`/`release`/`read` API, and the CAS helpers. **This child
ADDS `renew()` plus the lease-expiry/steal logic to that module** — it is not standalone and C1
must be merged first. (`renew()` is introduced here, not in C1.)

- **Relative-duration, skew-proof lease (DynamoDB-lock-client pattern):** expiry is NOT a
  cross-clone timestamp comparison. A contender records `(observed_oid, fence)`, waits one lease
  interval measured on its **own** clock, re-reads; if `fence` and the ref oid are unchanged, the
  holder made no progress for a full lease -> considered dead. This is why the stored `heartbeat_ns`
  is only diagnostic and the design is skew-proof.
- **`renew()`** (heartbeat) CAS-updates the blob's `heartbeat_ns` and increments `fence`
  (`fence += 1`) while the holder still owns the ref (old = observed oid). `fence` is a **monotonic
  progress-witness / generation counter** — it is the signal the expiry rule reads; it is NOT a
  full fencing token handed to the protected resource (no stale-writer rejection is claimed).
- **CAS-break-on-expiry:** a contender may steal **only** an expired lease, by CAS-replacing the
  blob against the exact observed OID. Because the steal is a compare-and-swap on the observed oid,
  a **double-break race resolves to exactly one winner** — the loser's CAS fails, it backs off and
  does NOT also acquire.
- **Lease + cadence config:** default lease **120 s**, heartbeat interval **`max(1, lease // 3)`**
  seconds (integer floor, not a vague `~lease/3`), read from a new `[reconciler]` key
  `lock_lease_secs`. Registering that key is C3's job (the `_config_schema.py` wiring lands with the
  `lock_backend` switch); here it is consumed with a safe default so C2 is testable standalone.
- **`renew()` failure contract:** a rejected renewal CAS means the lease was lost/stolen. `renew()`
  raises a **named exception** (e.g. `LeaseLostError`) — it does NOT silently retry into another
  holder's lock. The orchestrator (C3) turns that exception into a pass-abort.
- Structured log emission for lease-steal, renewal-failure, and expiry-detection events.
- Lives in `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_ref_lock.py` (extends C1); no reconciler
  orchestration wiring (that is C3). Extend the C1 ADR with the lease/heartbeat/fence contract.

## Design decisions (resolves plan-review ambiguities; binding on the implementation)

1. **API surface added to `_ref_lock.py`.** `renew(repo_root, ref, *, oid, remote=None) -> str`;
   and the steal is split into a **testable core** `try_break_if_stale(repo_root, ref, *, first,
   holder, remote=None) -> str | None` plus a convenience `steal(repo_root, ref, *, holder,
   remote=None, sleep_fn=time.sleep) -> str | None` that does observe -> `sleep_fn(first.lease_secs)`
   -> `try_break_if_stale`. The one-lease wait is injected via `sleep_fn` so unit tests exercise the
   CAS/expiry logic **without real time**; a short configured lease also keeps any live test fast.
2. **`renew()` contract.** Reads the current state; if the ref is absent or no longer points at
   `oid` -> `LeaseLostError` (no CAS attempted). Otherwise it CAS-updates the blob to
   `{holder, lease_secs (unchanged), heartbeat_ns=time.time_ns(), fence+1}` against `oid` (via the
   shared `_cas_once` seam / `--force-with-lease` on the remote path); a rejected CAS ->
   `LeaseLostError`. Returns the new oid the caller threads into the next `renew`. It NEVER retries
   into another holder's lock.
3. **Steal contract.** `try_break_if_stale` takes the `first` observation and re-reads: if the ref
   is now free (`None`) or its `(oid, fence)` changed -> returns `None` (the holder released or made
   progress = live, never stolen). Otherwise it CAS-replaces the blob against `first.oid` with
   `{holder=<contender>, lease_secs=first.lease_secs, heartbeat_ns=now, fence=first.fence + 1}`; a
   rejected CAS (lost the single-winner race) -> `None`. It never steals a **free** ref (that is
   `acquire`) and never steals a **live/renewing** holder.
4. **Fail-closed during steal/renew.** A `RefLockCorruptError` / `RefLockTimeoutError` from any read
   in the steal/renew path propagates (fail-closed = treat as HELD); a contender never steals on an
   unreadable blob.
5. **Lease constants.** `DEFAULT_LEASE_SECS = 120` and `heartbeat_interval(lease) -> max(1,
   int(lease // 3))` live in `_ref_lock.py`; C3 wires `[reconciler] lock_lease_secs` into the
   `acquire` call. `lease_secs` is carried IN the blob, so a contender waits the **holder's** lease,
   not its own — the skew-proof relative-duration rule.
6. **Structured logs.** Emit on lease-steal (won a break), expiry-detection (no `(oid, fence)`
   progress across one lease), and renewal-failure (`LeaseLostError`).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `_ref_lock.py` gains `renew()` (owner-only CAS-update of `heartbeat_ns`, `fence += 1`, against
      the held oid) and a lease-expiry evaluation using observed-no-progress-over-one-locally-measured
      -lease, not an absolute cross-clone clock diff; `renew()` on a lost/stolen lease raises
      `LeaseLostError` (no silent retry) and returns the new oid on success.
- [ ] A contender CAS-breaks only an expired lease (no `fence`/oid progress across one lease); a
      live, heartbeating holder is never stolen from (regression test); a free ref is never "stolen"
      (that path is `acquire`).
- [ ] Double-break race: two contenders race to steal one expired lock (both call
      `try_break_if_stale` against the same `first`); exactly one wins, the other observes CAS
      failure and returns `None` (does NOT acquire) — single-winner regression test.
- [ ] Lease = `[reconciler] lock_lease_secs` (default `DEFAULT_LEASE_SECS = 120`), heartbeat interval
      `heartbeat_interval(lease) = max(1, lease // 3)`, not hardcoded at the call sites; the one-lease
      wait is injectable (`sleep_fn`) so tests need no real sleep.
- [ ] A corrupt/unreadable blob encountered during steal/renew fails closed (treated as HELD), never
      stolen.
- [ ] Lease-steal / renewal-failure / expiry-detection events emit structured logs; the C1 ADR is
      extended with the lease/heartbeat/fence contract. Non-integration suite green.
compiled_state.title: Lease + heartbeat + CAS-break-on-stale self-healing (single-winner)
